name: Poached Salmon with Lobster Butter
recipeInstructions:
Note: Reduced lobster stock is made from shells and heads, then heavily reduced to 3-4 tablespoons.
Scale, gut, and wipe the fish. Rub the inside cavity with salt. If you have a fish kettle, steam the salmon whole over boiling water for 30-35 minutes (making sure the water is kept topped up). Alternatively, wrap the fish in lightly oiled foil and bake it in the oven at 325°F (170°C) gas mark 3 for an hour. Let it rest while you make the butter.
Warm the lemon juice in a small bowl over simmering water in a saucepan. Beat in cold butter cubes, adding more as each melts gently—do not overheat (revive with a quick splash of cold water). Once smooth and creamy like thin cream, strain and whisk in the lobster's green goo from its head, which turns the butter an orange-pink color.
Serve the salmon when it is cool but still juicy, flavored by its own juices. Accompany with hot baked potatoes and warm lobster butter.
